preview.tournamentOverview: TPC Twin Cities is a parkland layout that rewards ball-striking and approach play. SG APP (25.8%) and SG Total (22.8%) carry the heaviest weights, while SG PUTT (16.8%) and SG ATG (13.3%) also matter. The 3M Open is a mid-tier PGA Tour stop that offers strong value for in-form ball-strikers.
preview.modelExplainer: The model tilts heavily toward approach play and total strokes gained. SG APP at 25.8% and SG Total at 22.8% dominate, followed by SG PUTT (16.8%) and SG ATG (13.3%). Driving Accuracy (3.9%) and Bogey Avoidance (3.0%) receive minimal emphasis this week.
preview.pickApproach: We tier bets by model rank and power rank alignment. Top-10 model names with strong SG APP and SG Total are prioritized for outrights. Mid-tier names with SG APP edges are targeted for top-20 and top-40 matchups.

article.title: 3M Open Model Targets at TPC Twin Cities
article.dek: Model ranks place Jake Knapp and Scottie Scheffler at the top, with several mid-tier names showing large rank gaps versus power ratings.
article.introduction: The tournament model ranks Jake Knapp first and Scottie Scheffler second entering the 144-player field at TPC Twin Cities. Course weights emphasize SG APP at 25.8 percent and SG Total at 22.8 percent. Several players post tournament ranks well ahead of their power ranks, creating the primary angles for this week.

sections.heading: Tournament Overview
sections.body: TPC Twin Cities weights SG APP highest at 25.8 percent, followed by SG Total at 22.8 percent and SG PUTT at 16.8 percent. The model surface favors players who combine approach play with total strokes-gained volume.

sections.heading: Course Fit
sections.body: Jake Knapp posts the top SG Total of 2.002 and pairs it with SG PUTT 1.285, aligning with the 16.8 percent putting weight. Scottie Scheffler balances SG OTT 0.623 and SG ATG 0.618, covering the 11.4 percent off-the-tee and 13.3 percent around-the-green allocations.

sections.heading: Model Strength vs Power Rank
sections.body: Hideki Matsuyama sits at model rank 4 versus power rank 16, a 12-spot differential driven by SG APP 0.635. David Lipsky ranks fifth in the model against power rank 20, supported by SG ATG 0.524. Maverick McNealy lands at model rank 6 versus power rank 22 with SG Total 0.994.

sections.heading: Outright Targets
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler at model rank 2 carries SG Total 1.868 but faces high FedExCup rank 1 motivation risk. Hideki Matsuyama at model rank 4 trails the top two by more than 0.6 strokes in SG Total. David Lipsky at model rank 5 sits at FedExCup rank 76 on the bubble with zero tracked rounds. Maverick McNealy at model rank 6 carries a missed-cut Open finish and two tracked rounds.

sections.heading: Top-5 Targets
sections.body: Jake Knapp leads at model rank 1 with SG APP 0.342 that sits below the 25.8 percent course weight emphasis. Scottie Scheffler, Hideki Matsuyama, David Lipsky, and Maverick McNealy repeat from the outright tier with the same model and FedExCup qualifiers.

sections.heading: Top-10 Targets
sections.body: Jake Knapp at model rank 1 shows SG PUTT 1.285 that could regress if approach volume cools. Scottie Scheffler logged six tracked rounds across the last two weeks. Hideki Matsuyama posts SG OTT -0.099 against the 3.9 percent driving-accuracy weight. Rasmus Højgaard at model rank 10 carries SG ATG -0.273, the lowest mark among the top-10 model group. Jason Day at model rank 11 records SG APP -0.352, the weakest approach figure in the top 25.

sections.heading: Top-20 Targets
sections.body: David Lipsky at model rank 5 owns SG OTT -0.31, the lowest tee-to-green mark in the top 25. Maverick McNealy at model rank 6 shows SG APP 0.176 that remains modest relative to course weight. Austin Smotherman at model rank 7 posts SG PUTT -0.494, the weakest putting figure in the top 25. Andrew Putnam at model rank 8 records SG OTT -0.341. Sam Stevens at model rank 9 carries SG PUTT -0.218. Gary Woodland at model rank 15 owns SG ATG -0.397. Christiaan Bezuidenhout at model rank 16 shows both SG OTT -0.306 and SG APP -0.027 in negative territory.

sections.heading: Crossover Research Angles
sections.body: Hideki Matsuyama skipped the Scottish Open and lands at model rank 4 versus power rank 16. David Lipsky skipped the Scottish Open and sits at model rank 5 versus power rank 20. Maverick McNealy missed the Open cut, logged two tracked rounds, and ranks sixth in the model. Jake Knapp missed the Open cut and holds model rank 1. Rasmus Højgaard made the Scottish cut at T36 yet still ranks tenth in the model. Jason Day skipped the Scottish Open and sits at model rank 11 versus power rank 40.

sections.heading: Data Limitations
sections.body: Weather and tee-time data are unavailable. Workload coverage includes only recorded rounds from The Open Championship and Genesis Scottish Open; opposite-field, DP World Tour, LIV, and other events may be missing, so zero tracked rounds does not necessarily indicate rest.

sections.heading: Final Betting Card
sections.body: Strong model-supported tier: Jake Knapp (rank 1) and Scottie Scheffler (rank 2). Secondary tier: Hideki Matsuyama (rank 4), David Lipsky (rank 5), Maverick McNealy (rank 6). Speculative tier: Rasmus Højgaard (rank 10), Jason Day (rank 11), Austin Smotherman (rank 7), Andrew Putnam (rank 8), Sam Stevens (rank 9), Gary Woodland (rank 15), Christiaan Bezuidenhout (rank 16).

article.conclusion: Primary model plays sit with Jake Knapp and Scottie Scheffler; secondary exposure targets Hideki Matsuyama, David Lipsky, and Maverick McNealy on rank-gap support.

keyTakeaways.text: Jake Knapp leads the model at rank 1 with SG PUTT 1.285, the highest putting mark among the top 20.
Scottie Scheffler sits at model rank 2 with SG OTT 0.623 and SG ATG 0.618, both top-five values in the field.
Hideki Matsuyama ranks fourth in the model despite a power rank of 16, a 12-spot gap that highlights approach strength at SG APP 0.635.
David Lipsky and Maverick McNealy occupy model ranks 5 and 6 with SG Total marks of 0.89 and 0.994, both ahead of their power ranks by double digits.

methodologyNotes: Picks are generated from a course-weighted strokes-gained model, cross-checked against the official PGA TOUR field.
Post-Open Championship, Scottish Open, and FedExCup context is included because this is the tournament immediately following The Open Championship.
dataLimitations: Weather and tee-time data are not available in this pipeline; the article does not make weather- or tee-time-specific claims.

Workload coverage includes only recorded rounds from The Open Championship and Genesis Scottish Open. Opposite-field, DP World Tour, LIV, and other events may be missing, so “No tracked rounds” does not necessarily mean the player rested.
